title: AutoMobile Test Plan
description: Schema for AutoMobile test plan YAML files used with executePlan and JUnit Runner

name.description: Unique identifier for the test plan

description.description: Human-readable description of what this test plan does

devices.description: List of device labels for multi-device test execution. Required when using device parameters or criticalSection.

steps.description: Ordered list of tool execution steps

mcpVersion.description: MCP server version this plan was created with

generated.description: DEPRECATED: ISO 8601 timestamp (legacy field, use metadata.createdAt instead)

appId.description: DEPRECATED: Application bundle ID (legacy field, use metadata.appId instead)
Application bundle ID or package name

toolCallCount.description: Number of tool calls in original session

duration.description: Duration of original session in milliseconds

generatedFromToolCalls.description: Whether this plan was auto-generated from tool call logs

featureFlags.description: Feature flag values during plan creation

tool.description: Name of the MCP tool to execute

params.description: Tool-specific parameters (can also be specified as top-level properties)

device.description: Device label indicating which device this step runs on (required in multi-device plans for non-device-agnostic tools)

label.description: Human-readable description of what this step does

expectations.description: Assertions to validate after step execution

planStep.description: A step can have tool-specific parameters either in a 'params' object or as top-level properties (e.g., 'selector', 'appId', 'text', 'direction', etc.)

selector.description: Selector for the element to check

lock.description: Global lock identifier for synchronization across devices

steps.description: Steps to execute serially within the critical section

deviceCount.description: Expected number of devices that must reach the barrier before execution proceeds

criticalSectionParams.description: Parameters for criticalSection tool that provides barrier synchronization and mutex-based serial execution across multiple devices
